summary: Ownership chain: Dialog (society/brand) -> operated by Stonebrick, LLC (for-profit) -> owned by Auren Hoffman. Thiel is co-founder/namesake backer, NOT the documented legal owner. ED Raffi Grinberg + managing director Juliette Levine run ops. Fees graded A/B/C: lower-graded pay full price (>$10k) ~70% of the time; concrete data point = 2022 CA invitation showed $16,846 fee reduced ~70% by a discount code. 1,000+ paying members (target ~1,300 by end-2026), 2,500+ lifetime. Reporting explicitly says 'Dialog does not have a money-making business model' -> implies subsidy, but NO specific outside funders documented. Thiel Capital/Founders Fund/a16z NOT documented as funders (unverified). Thiel Foundation tied only via analyst commentary to the VA campus build-out (not a documented grant; its 990s are public but don't itemize Dialog). DC-area campus: reported Aug 2025; status conflicting (Axios/Semafor 'purchased' vs implicator.ai/Byline 'in negotiation'); cost/financing/acreage/buyer entity NOT reported — genuine documentation gap. Describes structure only; no wrongdoing alleged.

items.value_or_detail: Stonebrick, LLC — for-profit LLC operating Dialog; houses Auren Hoffman's business concerns. No disclosed nonprofit/501(c) status or 990 for Dialog itself.
Stonebrick LLC owned by co-founder Auren Hoffman; chain runs Dialog -> Stonebrick LLC -> Hoffman. Thiel co-founder/backer, not documented legal owner.
Raffi Grinberg = ED; Juliette Levine = managing director/staff (signed leak-notification). Hoffman chairman/owner; Thiel co-founder.
Grades A/B/C. Per this agent's sourcing C=highest-status/most famous, A=lowest, B=most members. Grade sets fee/discount. (Direction disputed across outlets.)
Lower-graded attendees pay full price, 'which can be more than $10,000'; higher-graded receive discounts.
2022 California-event invitation listed $16,846 registration fee, reduced ~70% via a discount code sent to the invitee.
Desirable/high-grade attendees can pay nothing; some had travel and lodging covered (e.g. ex-MEP Marietje Schaake recalled paying no registration fee).
'Dialog does not have a money-making business model' despite collecting graded fees — implies subsidy, but subsidizing entity not named.
1,000+ paying members (target ~1,300 by end-2026); 2,500+ lifetime participants over 20 years.

items.item: Thiel Capital / Founders Fund / a16z funding
items.value_or_detail: Not documented as Dialog funders. No reporting substantiates VC-firm bankrolling. Treat as unverified.
Cited by one analyst as playing a role in Dialog's shift to institutional phase (VA campus). Not a documented grant; Foundation 990s public via ProPublica but not shown to itemize Dialog.
Northern Virginia, just outside DC; commentary places it near Langley/the Pentagon. Shift from nomadic resort venues to permanent hub.
Conflicting: Axios/Semafor (Aug 2025) report land 'purchased'; implicator.ai and June-2026 Byline report 'active negotiation / ongoing'.

items.item: Permanent campus — cost / financing / buyer entity
items.value_or_detail: Not publicly reported. No purchase price, financing structure, acreage, or named buyer LLC disclosed.

items.item: Nonprofit 990 / state incorporation / DBA for Dialog
items.value_or_detail: No 501(c) determination, 990, state registration, or DBA for 'Dialog' surfaced; operates via Stonebrick LLC with near-zero public footprint.
